titleStmt.title: The Ambassador in the United Kingdom ( (Winant) ) to the Secretary of State

div.head: The Ambassador in the United Kingdom ( Winant ) to the Secretary of State
div.p: 3187. Deles No. 20. From the Under Secretary.The Under Secretary of State, Edward R. Stettinius, Jr., was head of a mission to London which held conversations from April 7 to April 29, 1944, with members of the British Government, with officials of Allied Governments, and with United States diplomatic, military, and civilian officials. Please send copy to the War Shipping Administration.

Reed,Philip Reed, Chief of Mission for Economic Affairs, American Embassy, London. PrattJohn L. Pratt, Consultant on Commercial Affairs, Department of State; member of the Stettinius Mission to London. and I have had an exploratory discussion with Lord LeathersBritish Minister of War Transport. concerning the British proposal contained in a memorandum
of March 7 submitted by British Embassy to Department in regard to the international control of shipping after the termination of the war in Europe. I suggest that Reed be designated as the United States representative to discuss with Lord Leathers the suggested revisions of the British memorandum put forward by WSA and approved by the Department, so far as they concern operational matters. Reed has already been authorized to join with Lord Leathers in discussions with certain of the Allied Ministers regarding the disposition of Allied ships found in Continental ports,See vol. iii, pp. 140 ff. and the two problems are of course related. If you and Admiral LandAdm. Emory S. Land, War Shipping Administrator; Chairman, Maritime Commission; American representative at Washington on Combined Shipping Adjustment Board. approve, please advise and forward appropriate instructions to Reed to guide him in his discussions, if any are needed to supplement the information in Department’s 2519 of March 31. [Stettinius.]
